# UC-22.50.41 · UN R155 7.2.2.5 — Cyber attack monitoring: VSOC detection and response evidence

## Description

Monitors VSOC detection and incident response rates for vehicle cyber attacks, providing evidence of UN R155 7.2.2.5 capabilities for monitoring, detecting, and responding to cyber threats.

## Value

UN R155 requires OEMs to monitor, detect, and respond to cyber attacks across their vehicle fleet. Continuous VSOC monitoring evidence demonstrates operational security capabilities.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest VSOC detection feeds and vehicle IDS alerts; (2) correlate with incident response tickets; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate low response rates. Indexes required: `index=incident_response`, `index=vsoc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=vehicle_ids`, `sourcetype=vehicle_security_event`, `sourcetype=vsoc_ir_tickets`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure data sources: VSOC detection feeds, vehicle telematics security events, in-vehicle IDS/IPS alerts, incident response ticket logs.
• See docs/implementation-guide.md for app layout and inputs.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest VSOC detection feeds and vehicle IDS alerts; (2) correlate with incident response tickets; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate low response rates. Indexes required: `index=incident_response`, `index=vsoc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=vehicle_ids`, `sourcetype=vehicle_security_event`, `sourcetype=vsoc_ir_tickets`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL (save as report or alert; tune time range and thresholds):

```spl
(index=vsoc sourcetype IN (vehicle_ids, vehicle_security_event)) OR (index=incident_response sourcetype=vsoc_ir_tickets) earliest=-30d
| eval detected=if(sourcetype IN ("vehicle_ids", "vehicle_security_event") AND isnotnull(threat_category), 1, 0)
| eval responded=if(sourcetype="vsoc_ir_tickets" AND status IN ("acknowledged", "investigating", "resolved"), 1, 0)
| stats sum(detected) AS detections sum(responded) AS responses dc(vehicle_model) AS models_monitored dc(threat_category) AS threat_categories BY attack_vector
| eval response_rate=if(detections>0, round(100*responses/detections, 1), 0)
| where response_rate<95 OR detections=0
| table attack_vector, detections, responses, response_rate, models_monitored, threat_categories
```

Understanding this SPL

**UN R155 7.2.2.5 — Cyber attack monitoring: VSOC detection and response evidence** — UN R155 requires OEMs to monitor, detect, and respond to cyber attacks across their vehicle fleet. Continuous VSOC monitoring evidence demonstrates operational security capabilities.

Output fields and breakdowns should match the panel fields listed in **Visualization**.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with the crosswalk for the tier-2 framework clause, so monitoring evidence matches the mapped control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a compliance or VSOC dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Consider: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `attack_vector`, `detected`, `detections`, `models_monitored`, `responded`, `response_rate`.

## SPL

```spl
(index=vsoc sourcetype IN (vehicle_ids, vehicle_security_event)) OR (index=incident_response sourcetype=vsoc_ir_tickets) earliest=-30d
| eval detected=if(sourcetype IN ("vehicle_ids", "vehicle_security_event") AND isnotnull(threat_category), 1, 0)
| eval responded=if(sourcetype="vsoc_ir_tickets" AND status IN ("acknowledged", "investigating", "resolved"), 1, 0)
| stats sum(detected) AS detections sum(responded) AS responses dc(vehicle_model) AS models_monitored dc(threat_category) AS threat_categories BY attack_vector
| eval response_rate=if(detections>0, round(100*responses/detections, 1), 0)
| where response_rate<95 OR detections=0
| table attack_vector, detections, responses, response_rate, models_monitored, threat_categories
```

## Visualization

Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `attack_vector`, `detected`, `detections`, `models_monitored`, `responded`, `response_rate`.

## References

- [UN Regulation No. 155 — Cyber Security](https://unece.org/transport/documents/2021/03/standards/un-regulation-no-155-cyber-security-and-cyber-security)
